title: Turn an Anomaly into a Question
one_sentence: Use a surprising fact as the starting point for a research question rather than as a result to decorate.

title: Find Variation in Institutions
one_sentence: Look for rules, thresholds, timing, or assignments that create informative variation.

title: Isolate the Core Friction
one_sentence: Reduce a complex setting to the friction that drives behavior and predictions.

title: Build a Minimal Model
one_sentence: Use the simplest model that clarifies the mechanism and produces testable predictions.

title: Derive Testable Predictions
one_sentence: Turn a theory into predictions that can be contradicted by data.

title: Separate Competing Mechanisms
one_sentence: Design tests that distinguish your explanation from plausible alternatives.

title: Find Identification in Institutions
one_sentence: Use institutional rules, timing, or constraints to create credible comparisons.

title: Design a Falsification Test
one_sentence: Test where the theory predicts no effect or a different effect.

title: Measure an Unobservable Concept
one_sentence: Build and validate proxies for beliefs, constraints, quality, attention, or institutions.

title: Test the Mechanism, Not Only the Effect
one_sentence: Show why the main result happens rather than only documenting that it happens.

title: Separate Risk from Mispricing
one_sentence: Design evidence that distinguishes compensation for risk from mistakes, beliefs, constraints, or limits to arbitrage.

title: Use Prices as Belief Measures
one_sentence: Treat asset prices, returns, and spreads as noisy but informative objects about expectations, risk, and constraints.

title: Connect Treatment Effects to Welfare
one_sentence: Move from estimating effects to explaining welfare, mechanisms, and policy relevance.

title: Use Policy Variation as Design
one_sentence: Convert policy rules, timing, eligibility, or reforms into credible research designs.

title: Write the One-Paragraph Paper
one_sentence: State the question, answer, design, mechanism, and contribution in one coherent paragraph.

title: Anti-Skill: Overclaiming from Weak Identification
one_sentence: Avoid making causal or mechanism claims that the design cannot support.

title: Anti-Skill: Confusing Interesting with Important
one_sentence: Avoid mistaking novelty or cleverness for a contribution that changes beliefs.

title: Anti-Skill: Adding Robustness without Learning
one_sentence: Avoid adding checks that do not clarify threats, mechanisms, or interpretation.
